what_it_does: This JSON file is an interpretive ledger entry for the Templates.jsx React canvas. It records a neutral, file-local analysis of what the component does, how it is structured, the patterns it follows, its standout behaviors, concerns, open questions, themes, and dense embedding summaries.
observations: The file follows a fixed interpretive schema with keys such as project, path, model, what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, summary_for_embedding, and intention_for_embedding.
It documents a React canvas named Templates that renders saved Build/Workshop runs as reusable, parameterised templates.
It notes that the component aliases React hooks (useState_tp, useMemo_tp, useEffect_tp) to avoid bundle-scope collisions.
It records that the component subscribes to CV_REGISTRY changes and re-decorates templates when the registry updates.
It describes two layouts: flat grid and grouped-by-parent-Type sections.
It distinguishes workshop-doc templates (using t.variables and WS_AI.materializeTemplate) from brief templates (using t.params plus a briefPattern resolved by resolveBrief).
It highlights a 'fauxType' construction that merges a parent Type with the template's doc to drive a TypeThumb preview.
It flags heavy use of inline style objects referencing CSS custom properties.
It lists three explicit questions about CV_REGISTRY.subscribe, saved-template object shape, and WS_AI.materializeTemplate.
It contains no code symbols and marks symbols as an empty list.
standouts: Templates are modeled as instances/children of registry Types, with every filled parameter framed as a registered Variable on the parent.
resolveBrief leaves unresolved {{key}} placeholders intact rather than blanking them.
A 'fauxType' merge lets a saved template render a live thumbnail through the same TypeThumb engine used for real Types.
conventions: Follows the ledger's interpretive schema and neutral, file-local, non-ranking tone.
Uses embedding-oriented summary and intention fields for downstream retrieval.
Mirrors the described component's adherence to a registry-projection principle and a bundle no-import pattern.
Notes a deviation in the described component: heavy inline style objects rather than CSS classes, even though values are tokenised via var().
concerns: The described component relies on optional globals (WS_AI, TypeThumb, TypeLayerBadge, CV_REGISTRY) with optional chaining; absence could silently degrade behavior.
The correctness of the useEffect cleanup depends on CV_REGISTRY.subscribe returning an unsubscribe function.
The described component duplicates styling logic across many inline style objects.
This ledger entry itself leaves the exact contracts of globals and saved-template schemas unresolved.
notes: An interpretive record for the Templates canvas, summarising its registry-driven gallery of reusable templates, editable-variable cards, and Type-anchored reuse model.
questions: What is the exact schema/contract for CV_REGISTRY entries referenced in the analysis?
How are saved templates persisted, loaded, and what is their full object shape?
What does WS_AI.materializeTemplate return on success and how is it consumed?
Is the heavy use of inline style objects in the described component an intentional theming choice or a maintainability concern?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file states that the header comment's described purpose aligns with the implemented behavior.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-truth: templates derive grouping and structure from the Type Registry.
Parameterised reuse: saved runs become forkable templates via editable variables.
Types-to-Templates lineage: a template is treated as a child of a doc Type.
UI as a projection of the registry.
intention_signals: To turn ad-hoc generative runs into a structured, reusable library where each template is anchored to a registry Type, editable by its declared variables, and re-runnable to compose fresh variants while preserving lineage.
novelty: The interpretive framing of saved runs as registered Type-children with editable Variables, plus the fauxType merge that gives a saved template a live registry-style thumbnail.
contribution: Contributes a neutral interpretive documentation of the Templates canvas: its registry-derived grouping, TemplateCard semantics, workshop-doc versus brief-template branching, resolveBrief placeholder handling, fauxType thumbnail mechanism, and dense embedding summaries for downstream reasoning.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger interpretive record for the Templates.jsx canvas component describing its registry-driven reusable-template gallery, editable variables, brief-placeholder resolver, and Type-anchored reuse model.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the design intent and functional model of the Templates canvas so fused systems can retrieve, reason about, and preserve its registry-projection and reuse semantics.
